title: AHP-express: A simplified version of the analytical hierarchy process method
author: Leal

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: The method is based on the assumption of evaluation consistency; it also relies on the hypothesis that inconsistency occurs mainly in evaluations between alternatives of seemingly minor importance to the decisionmaker. By taking as its basis alternatives of apparent greater importance, the method makes a more careful analysis when comparing these alternatives with the other ones. Thus, it is proposed that for each calculation of priorities in each criterion, the alternative that the decisionmaker considers the most or one of the most important alternatives is taken as the basis of comparison. Thereafter, the comparison is made using the evaluation scale proposed by Saaty between this alternative and all other alternatives, and then, the elements of the vector of priorities can be calculated by means of formula (4).(4)prj=1aij*∑k1aik where j is the element for which one wishes to calculate the priority, i is the element taken as the basis for the comparison, aij is the comparison value of alternative i with alternative j, and prj is the priority of alternative j against the criterion considered. The development of this formula is found in the supplementary material along with a proof that this formula gives the elements of the eigenvector associated with the main eigenvalue, in the case of consistency in the matrix.

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: The following are the steps for the simplified method.1Set the main purpose of the decision-making process.2Define the secondary objectives that together meet the primary objective at the second level of the structure of priorities.3For each objective at the second level, if necessary, define third-level objectives that meet the next higher objective.4Define the alternatives to be considered at the lower level.5For each element in a level, repeat the following:aSet the element of apparent greater importance with respect to the criterion of the higher level.bApply formula 4 to calculate the elements of the vector of priorities for the criterion under consideration.6Calculate the priorities of each alternative within each criterion ascending in the tree to the main objective.

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: This method can also be applied for the pairwise comparisons used in the analytical network process (ANP, [8]).

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: The procedure can be represented in matrix form.

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: Define:

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: a: alternative a, a = 1,…,na

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: Ci: criterion i, i = 1,…,nc

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: SCi,j: subcriterion j within criterion i, j = 1,…,nsi

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: For each criterion i, we calculate the following:

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: A vector PSCi of priorities of subcriterion j in criterion i:(6)PSCi=cg1i…cgnsii

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: A matrix PASC i of priorities of alternative a in each subcriterion j of criterion i:(7)PASCi=pasc1,1i..pascna,1i...pasc1,nsii.pascna,ns ii

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: Each matrix PASCi can be ordered by each criterion i grouped in a matrix MPASC:(8)MPASC=pasc1,1i…pascna,1i………pasc1,nsii…pascna,ns ii………pasc1,1ncpascna,1nc………pasc1,nsncncpascna,nsncnc

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: Each vector PSCi can be included in a matrix MPSC of priorities of subcriteria, which is partitioned to correspond to the priorities of alternatives in matrix MPASC:(9)MPASC=cg11cgns110..000000cg12..cgns2200................000000cg1nccgnsncnc

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: The product of MPSC by PASC gives the matrix PAC of priorities for each alternative a for criteria i. Each row of matrix PAC correspond to a vector of priorities of alternatives in each criteria i.(10)MPSC*PASC=PAC=pac1,1..pacna,1......pac1,nc..pacna,nc

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: Further, we calculate a vector PC of priorities of each criterion for the objective.(11)PC=pc1..pcnc

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: The product of PC and PAC gives the vector PA of final priorities for each alternative for the objective.(12)PA=PC*PAC=p1…pna

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: In our example, we have four alternatives (A1, A2, A3 and A4), two criteria (C1 and C2) and two subcriteria for each criterion (C11, C12, C21 and C22).

steps_raw.instruction: The calculation of the priorities for the alternatives of each subcriteria, using Formula 4, are presented in Table 2, Table 3, Table 4, Table 5.Table 2Priority calculation for subcriteria SC11.Table 2Table 3Priority calculation for subcriteria SC12.Table 3Table 4Priority calculation for subcriteria SC21.Table 4Table 5Priority calculation for subcriteria SC22.Table 5

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: For each subcriterion, we can group the priorities of the alternatives in a matrix:PASCi=pasc1,1i..pascna,1i...pasc1,nsii.pascna,ns ii

steps_raw.instruction: For subcriteria C11 and C12 of criterion 1, we have:

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: A vector (PSCi) of the priorities of subcriterion j in criterion i is calculated for each criterion (Table 6, Table 7):PSCi=cg1i…cgnsiiTable 6Matrix of priorities of the alternatives in the subcriteria of criterion 1.Table 6Table 7Matrix of the priorities of the alternatives in each subcriterion (PASC).Table 7

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: The calculation of the priorities for each subcriterion in each criterion is presented in Table 8, Table 9.Table 8Calculation of the priorities of the subcriteria of criterion 1.Table 8Table 9Calculation of the priorities of the subcriteria of criterion 2.Table 9

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: The priorities are grouped in a matrix PSC, which is partitioned to multiply the weights of the subcriteria in the corresponding criteria by the priorities of the alternatives in the corresponding subcriteria (Table 10).Table 10Matrix of the priorities of the subcriteria for each criterion (PSC).Table 10

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: The product of multiplying PSC by PASC (see the schema in Table 11) is the matrix PAC (Table 12), which contains the priorities for each alternative a for criterion i. Each row of the PAC matrix (Table 13) corresponds to a vector of the priorities of the alternatives for each criterion i.PSC*PASC=PAC=pac1,1..pacna,1......pac1,nc..pacna,ncTable 11Schema of the matrix multiplication PSC*PASC.Table 11Table 12Priorities of the alternatives in each criterion (PAC).Table 12Table 13Calculation of priorities of each criterion.Table 13

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: The result of this matrix multiplication is the matrix PAC, which will be multiplied by the vector PC of the priorities of the criteria as shown in Table 13:

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: The final priorities of the alternatives PA (Table 15) are calculated by the matrix multiplication PC*PAC following the schema shown in Table 14.Table 14Schema of the matrix calculation of the final priorities.Table 14Table 15Vector (PA) of the final priorities of the alternatives.Table 15

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: The diagram of Fig. 4 resumes the steps of the method for a hierarchy with criteria, subcriteria and alternatives. The box at the left calculates priorities for a given set of ne elements that can be alternatives, subcriteria or criteria with respect to an upper-level element in the hierarchy.Fig. 3AHP-express process in an Excel spreadsheet.Fig. 3Fig. 4Diagram with steps of Ahp-express for a hierarchy with criteria, subcriteria and alternatives.Fig. 4

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: The Microsoft Excel worksheet in Fig. 3 shows the entire calculation process. The main formulas are shown in the figure.

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: First, the priority is calculated for each alternative in each subcriterion SC11, SC12, SC21 and SC22. One then calculates the PSC1 and PSC2 priorities of each subcriterion for each criterion and the priorities of each criterion for the final objective in the PC vector.

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: The vectors of priorities of each alternative in each subcriterion are sorted and grouped in the MPASC matrix. In turn, the subcriteria vectors are grouped into a partitioned MPSC matrix to match the rows of the MPASC matrix. The product of the MPSC matrix and the MPASC matrix gives the PAC matrix of priorities of the alternatives in each criterion, shown in rows 36–39. The result is premultiplied (rows 41 and 42) by the vector of priorities of the criteria and results in the final PA vector of priorities of each alternative for the final objective, row 44.

steps_raw.title: Steps of the AHP-express method
steps_raw.instruction: This finding indicates that for this decision-making process, the A1 alternative with priority 0.62 is dominant over the others, and the alternative A4 has the second priority, which is well below that of A1.
